title: 2 siblings drown in Vermont river, brother tried to save younger sister, police say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sandro Lala, 25, and Yazmin Yupangui, 11, drowned in Vermont's Lamoille River while fishing last week, police reported.
Lala attempted to rescue his sister who fell into the water; both were reported missing shortly after entering the river.
Yupangui's body was found Thursday evening; Lala's was discovered by a kayaker the following morning, investigations confirmed accidental drownings.

title: City declines to use reserve funds to prevent BPS layoffs as more than 560 positions are cut
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston Public Schools will cut more than 560 positions due to enrollment declines and a $1.7 billion budget.
City Council member Ed Flynn urged using reserve funds to prevent layoffs affecting predominantly lower-income communities.
The Wu administration states reserve funds should be for extraordinary costs, maintaining a 10:1 student-teacher ratio.

title: Harmful bacteria persist in Merrimack River days after Haverhill sewer break
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Merrimack River still tests high for bacteria like fecal coliform and E. coli after Haverhill sewage spill.
Four out of seven river sites show elevated bacteria, with highest readings at Plum Island Light.
Shellfish harvesting areas remain closed in multiple North Shore locations due to contamination concerns.

title: Boater remains missing after crash off of Salisbury Beach
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three people were involved in a boat crash off Salisbury Beach on Friday night; two were rescued, but one remains missing.
The missing man, identified by relatives as Vilmar Dorner, 49, from North Reading, remains unaccounted for after extensive search efforts.
The U.S. Coast Guard suspended the search for Dorner on Saturday evening after unsuccessful air and marine attempts.

title: After officiating Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding, Adam Sandler played pickup basketball in this Mass. vacation hotspot
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Adam Sandler officiated Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding at Madison Square Garden on July 4, 2026.
Sandler played pickup basketball in Nantucket on July 5, after requesting game locations from the local police.
Sandler is known for playing pickup basketball with locals and posing for photos after games.

title: Local obituary: Oliver Lewis, 15, ‘an old soul, with wisdom far beyond his years’
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Oliver Lewis, 15, of Franklin, Massachusetts, died on June 23, 2026, from injuries sustained in a bicycle accident.
Oliver, known for his kindness and wisdom, was an avid reader and excelled in academics and sports.
He saved five lives through organ donation and a scholarship fund was established in his memory at Bishop Feehan High School.

title: Poll: Most in Mass. think police hid evidence in Karen Read case
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A UNH poll shows 69% of Massachusetts residents believe police hid evidence in Karen Read's case.
Nearly two-thirds say police conduct in Read's case suggests broader policing issues in Massachusetts.
86% of Massachusetts residents support the right to sue police for rights violations, according to the poll.

title: Duxbury man drowns while swimming to his boat
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: James Merlin, 66, drowned while attempting to swim to his boat in Duxbury Bay on Sunday.
Bystanders pulled Merlin to shore and an off-duty officer administered CPR before he was taken to the hospital.
Authorities do not suspect foul play in the drowning incident, which is under investigation by state police.

title: Groveland ice cream shop goes viral with scoop-tossing video
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Jeff and Maria's Ice Cream in Groveland went viral with a video showing employees humorously tossing ice cream.
Owner Jeff Boragine continues to run the shop in memory of his late wife Maria, maintaining a fun work environment.
The viral video reached nearly 23 million views on Instagram, attracting visitors from hours away to the ice cream shop.

title: Celtics sign first-round pick Chris Cenac Jr.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Boston Celtics signed 19-year-old power forward Chris Cenac Jr., the No. 27 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.
Cenac, a McDonald's All-American, played one season at Houston, averaging 9.5 points and 7.9 rebounds.
Celtics see Cenac as a developmental project, bolstering frontcourt depth amid power forward needs.
